generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components (implied by physical-to-virtual interaction)
instance OperationalStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time operational parameters from the physical system including performance metrics and anomalies. Operates in near-real-time to support bidirectional data flows."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalStateDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregates contextual data traces such as environmental parameters and performance metrics from the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
]

instance HistoricalOperationalDataSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides access to historical operational logs and performance data for long-term analysis and trend detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HistoricalOperationalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OperationalStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalStateSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalParameterSensor
]

instance HistoricalOperationalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HistoricalOperationalDataSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured system requirements description using MBSE methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM). Defines functional and non-functional requirements for the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional architecture representation using SysML or ADLs (e.g., ACME, AADL) to describe system functions and their interactions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcADL
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Logical architecture model detailing component interfaces, interconnections, and composition using SysML IBDs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance ProductTechnicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Product/technical layer model incorporating geometric (CAD) and physics-based analytical representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance StateMachineBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using SysML v1.7 state machines or MontiArc Automaton to define dynamic system behavior and transitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance OperationalStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational parameters including performance metrics, anomalies, and system states aggregated from physical sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink, CppControlLogic
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual environmental parameters and performance metrics collected from physical system sensors."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcADL, JavaDataProcessor
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated historical operational logs and performance data for trend analysis and predictive modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HistoricalOperationalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataScienceAnalytics
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based framework for multi-layered abstraction (requirements, functional/logical architecture, product layer) and behavioral modeling (state machines)."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionSimulationService, VisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcADL : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Architecture Description Language for modeling components, connectors, interfaces, and composition with MontiArc Automaton for behavioral modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, ControlService
]

instance MathWorksSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation and control system design tool for detailed behavioral simulation and real-time processing of operational data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionSimulationService, ControlService
]

instance CppControlLogic : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ implementation of low-level control algorithms and real-time processing for physical system interaction."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance JavaDataProcessor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Java-based data processing and integration layer for harmonizing heterogeneous data sources across tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance DataScienceAnalytics : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data science tools and algorithms for predictive analytics, trend detection, and optimization using historical and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionSimulationService, OptimizationService
]

instance RFLPMethodology : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics (RFLP) methodology for managing complexity across abstraction layers and stakeholder perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionSimulationService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time or near-real-time state observation of the physical system via bidirectional data flows, enabling dynamic tracking of operational parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance PredictionSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Proactive analysis using integrated simulation tools to forecast system behavior, identify potential failures, or optimize operational scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, OptimizationScenarioInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous or semi-autonomous decision-making to adjust system parameters based on real-time data and model-driven insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction, ResourceAllocationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback mechanisms where the Digital Twin actively pushes commands back to the physical system to maintain desired states."
    DTDFVocab:provides StateCorrectionAction, CommandExecutionAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-perspective dashboards integrating SysML models, ADL diagrams, and simulation outputs for contextualized insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalArchitectureViewInsight, RealTimeTelemetryInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic tracking of operational parameters and system states in real-time for immediate situational awareness."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of performance anomalies or deviations from expected behavior using real-time data analysis."
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted system behavior and potential failure modes based on simulation and predictive analytics."
]

instance OptimizationScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized operational scenarios and parameter configurations derived from simulation and data-driven analysis."
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextualized view of the system's functional architecture integrating SysML models and ADL diagrams."
]

instance RealTimeTelemetryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry data visualization combining operational states, environmental parameters, and simulation outputs."
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous adjustment of system parameters (e.g., process variables, resource allocation) based on real-time insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ResourceAllocationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic resource allocation optimization to improve system efficiency and performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StateCorrectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Active correction of system states to maintain desired operational parameters via closed-loop feedback."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CommandExecutionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of control commands generated by the Digital Twin to actuate physical system changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system’s orchestration and coordination are implicitly tied to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which rely on **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements, functional, logical, and product/technical layers) and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, or SysML IBDs. These ADLs define **component & connector perspectives**, enabling **system-wide coordination** through explicit descriptions of components, interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral coordination is further supported by **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed behavior modeling may integrate **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink)** or **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)**. The document does not specify explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, service-oriented architecture) but implies a **modular, tool-agnostic integration framework** to manage system-wide coordination across heterogeneous models and stakeholders."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through **validation and verification (V&V) methods** aligned with MBSE practices. Model fidelity is ensured by **multi-layer abstraction** (e.g., requirements → functional → logical → physical layers) and **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** to systematically refine accuracy. **Uncertainty sources** are quantified via **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink, MathWorks)** and **analytical models**, with error detection mechanisms embedded in **state machines (SysML v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton** to monitor deviations. **Quality assurance processes** include **cross-tool consistency checks** (e.g., validating ADL-defined interfaces against simulation outputs) and **stakeholder integration** to reconcile heterogeneous perspectives. However, explicit details on **error correction mechanisms** or **quantitative uncertainty quantification** (e.g., tolerance thresholds) are not provided in the document."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document highlights the need for **horizontal integration** with external systems through **interoperability mechanisms** enabled by MBSE methodologies. Key aspects include:
  1. **Data exchange protocols**: Implicitly supported via **ADLs (e.g., ACME, AADL)** and **SysML IBDs**, which define standardized interfaces for component interconnection.
  2. **Interoperability standards**: The use of **SysML** (a standardized modeling language) and **integration with simulation tools (Simulink, other simulation methods)** suggests adherence to **modeling and simulation interoperability standards** (e.g., FMI, HLA, or OMG standards).
  3. **Dependencies on external systems**: The document emphasizes the necessity to integrate **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives**, implying dependencies on **enterprise systems, legacy tools, or other Digital Twins** via **shared data models or middleware solutions**.
  4. **Integration patterns**: The **component & connector** paradigm of ADLs facilitates **loose coupling** between DT instances and external systems, while **state machines or automata** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton) enable **event-driven coordination** across boundaries. The document does not specify exact protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) but aligns with **standardized, tool-agnostic integration** approaches."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** standards, leveraging **SysML** (Systems Modeling Language) as a core specification for system modeling across abstraction layers. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** provide standardized frameworks for component-based system design, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling is standardized via **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines (v1.7)**. Interoperability is supported through integration with **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) and **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java), ensuring compliance with broader engineering and software development standards. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (as per Kritzinger et al. [32]) defines foundational concepts for data aggregation and abstraction, though no explicit certification or compliance frameworks are mentioned in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the implementation of a **safety cell** that restricts the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]**, ensuring compliance with predefined safety boundaries. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implicitly addressed via the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, which enforces positional constraints and limits maximum speed to **|q̇_max| = 75 [°/s]** to prevent mechanical overstress. **Threat mitigation strategies** are partially implied by the **dual encoder system** (motor encoder + secondary encoder post-gearbox), which compensates for **backlash and positional uncertainty** by cross-referencing encoder data to infer drive-train elasticity and friction. However, explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** are not detailed in the provided documents. **Risk assessment approaches** are not explicitly described beyond the safety cell's operational constraints."
]
